Reusable event class; distinct standard class alongside natural_disaster.
Reusable class (issuing shares/rights); canonical of pair.
'Dilution' is an effect-on-stock word (R7); same issuance risk factor as share_issuance (matching quotes); wording-only.
Singular/plural variant of same buyback-activity driver; canonical = 34-ref plural.
Distinct event class (board authorization increases); kept separate from buyback activity.
Reusable tax-cost class (excise tax on buybacks).
Reusable capital-return activity driver; 34 refs; canonical.
Reusable logistics event class.
Reusable competitive-marketing cause.
Reusable reputation cause (viral negative publicity); distinct from marketing.
Identical quote text ('increased restaurant staff absenteeism') as restaurant_staff_absenteeism; wording variant.
Attrition is the inverse metric of retention — polarity-flipping fold would not be wording-only, so kept separate.
Reusable workforce driver; 21 consistent refs.
Quote ('staffing challenges') names the same ability-to-staff cause as restaurant_staffing; same scope/mechanism.
Same 'improved staffing levels' evidence as restaurant_staffing's own refs; restaurant-scoped per context; wording variant.
Quote is about scheduled state wage mandates in a wage-inflation discussion = state minimum-wage increases; same object/scope/mechanism as minimum_wage.
Standard expense item; reusable.
Reusable supply-risk cause (few-vendor dependence).
Bare domain word, too broad (R7); its evidence is supply-chain stability/instability — same cause as supply_chain_disruption; wording-only fix.
Distinct cost variable (supply chain savings/costs).
Reusable event class; 35 refs; canonical.
Pure plural variant of supply_chain_disruption.
Single quote blends integration and scale benefits; not clearly identical to either sibling — kept separate per 3-check.
Distinct cause (integrating acquired concepts' purchasing).
Distinct cause (purchasing scale benefits).
Reusable class (product supply shortages); facet distinct from general disruption — kept separate.
Reusable regulatory class (GHG/sustainability requirements).
Distinct cost/price-volatility driver for sustainable products.
Distinct supply-availability driver; 3 consistent refs.
Same throughput object ('drive more throughput'/'wouldn't impact throughput'), same scope/mechanism as restaurant_throughput; wording variant.
Evidence's causal variable is the tariffs themselves (cost absorption = impact/state); same cause as tariff_policy (R5 familiar form).
Familiar policy driver (R5); 32 consistent refs.
Reusable event class.
Same object as tax_policy (changes in tax law/regulation affecting tax provision); same scope/mechanism; wording variant.
Familiar policy driver; 3 consistent refs; canonical.
Specific reusable cause (recouping landlord allowances via percentage rent).
Segment-specific revenue driver — valid per R9.
Reusable channel driver; 6 consistent refs.
Reusable class (delivery partnership agreements/terms); distinct from the channel itself.
Distinct cost driver (delivery cost increases).
Reusable causal variable: customer-paid third-party delivery fees affecting comparable sales; 3 consistent 10-K quotes name the fees as the cause; noun-only, rule-clean.
Reusable event class (vendor failure -> data loss, cost, operational disruption); 6 consistent quotes; class names are admissible even if recurring risk language; rule-clean.

Its single quote ('failure of third-party vendors to provide adequate services') is the exact same sentence as third_party_vendor_failure's 2023 evidence — same object (vendors), same scope, same mechanism. 'Outage' is an invented synonym not in the source; coined clean form exists. Wording-only fold.
Reusable regulatory wage variable (state tip-credit wage rate, incl. elimination risk); 4 quotes consistently name it; distinct from generic minimum wage; noun-only.
Reusable cost line: to-go item packaging named in transcript as a cost area (corroborated by same-call company quote on to-go related expenses); specific, noun-only.

Evidence is a single reported result vs prior year (no consensus, no outlook) — bare result metric with ambiguous mechanism breaks the earnings convention ({metric}_surprise/{metric}_guidance). Coined revenue_surprise/revenue_guidance exist in the catalog, but rewriting would assert a mechanism the evidence does not show (fails three-check), so skip.
Metric + mechanism per earnings convention: company's stated long-term total-shareholder-return objective (13-14%) is forward guidance on a named metric; reusable across restatements.
Familiar macro/policy driver (R5); transcript grounds it ('assuming... we get some trade deals' as an outlook assumption). Shutdown in same quote is a separate driver, correctly not bundled.
Reusable operational event class (transportation delays disrupting supply); grounded in 10-K wording; class admissible despite one occurrence; distinct mechanism from generic supply shortage.

Both its quotes describe 'a change to our reserve for uncertain tax positions' — the exact same object/scope/mechanism as uncertain_tax_positions (whose XBRL anchor us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefits IS that reserve). Wording-only variant of a coined standard phrase.
Standard financial phrase (R6), XBRL-anchored (us-gaap:UnrecognizedTaxBenefits); reusable tax-rate driver with consistent 8-K evidence; canonical form for the reserve variant.
Familiar macro variable; 13 consistent quotes ('changes in unemployment rates', low unemployment driving wage pressure); noun-only, rule-clean.

Reusable causal variable: pace of new restaurant openings/development (incl. CapEx supporting it); 4 transcript quotes about the same real-world pipeline. Kept separate from unit_development_guidance (different mechanism: activity vs outlook).

Metric + guidance mechanism per earnings convention; 3 quotes are explicit forward opening targets ('expects to open as many as X'). Separate driver from unit_development per actual-vs-guidance distinction.

Single vague quote ('the very urban locations... areas with the most opportunity') never names traffic — the important noun 'traffic' is not source-grounded (R10), and 'opportunity' could mean sales, margins, or development. Too vague to admit.
Reusable macro variable: USD value vs other currencies driving imported-goods costs; 4 consistent 10-K quotes; specific, noun-only.

Plural/spelling variant of coined utility_costs — same object (restaurant utility costs), same scope, same mechanism (utility prices pressuring operating margin); its single quote matches the richer record's meaning exactly. Wording-only fold per one-name-per-driver.
Reusable cost variable with 7 consistent quotes (utilities/nat-gas pressure on other operating expenses); canonical form for the utilities_costs variant; noun-only.
Same object (wage-and-hour class/collective actions), same scope, same mechanism as coined wage_hour_litigation; name contains banned glue word 'and' (R7). Wording-only fix to the coined clean form.
Reusable litigation class (wage-and-hour class actions); 2 quotes show recurring exposure; clean form without glue word; canonical for the _and_ variant.
Reusable labor-cost variable with 25 consistent quotes across 10-K/10-Q/transcripts, XBRL-anchored; standard phrase; canonical for wage_rate_inflation and wage_rates variants.

Its only quote ('menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation') is the same boilerplate sentence found repeatedly inside wage_inflation's evidence — same object, scope, mechanism. Wording-only variant of the coined canonical.

Despite the bare-level name, both its quotes literally say 'wage rate inflation' — identical sentences to wage_inflation's evidence; same object/scope/mechanism per evidence, so folding aligns the name with its own evidence without changing the driver.

Reusable causal variable (weather impacts on restaurant traffic/comps); 7 quotes consistently attribute traffic effects to weather; familiar short form; canonical for weather_conditions/weather_disruption variants.

All 9 quotes are 'adverse weather conditions' affecting sales/traffic — same object (weather), same scope (CAKE restaurant sales), same mechanism as coined weather; 'adverse' is state and 'conditions' is filler. Wording-only fold (natural disasters in the quotes are a separate variable, correctly not in this name).

Both quotes ('weather-related impacts' on revenue; 'adverse weather conditions... exacerbate' — the latter shared verbatim with weather_conditions) mean the same cause as coined weather; 'disruption' is an effect word that belongs in state. Wording-only fold.

Reusable event class (wildfires recur and hit regional traffic); evidence is one instance (LA fires) but the name is a class, not instance-bound; singular form matches ontology class style; canonical for the plural variant.

Pure plural variant of coined wildfire — both records cite the same LA-fires impact: same object, same scope, same mechanism. §2 resolves plural variants to one canonical form; singular matches ontology class style (government_shutdown, goodwill_impairment).

Reusable seasonal class ('weather in January' suppressing traffic — a recurring Q1 phenomenon); kept SEPARATE from weather because folding a narrower seasonal scope into the broad form would change scope (fails check 2), like the brand-vs-company R9 rule.
Reusable cost variable (workers' comp insurance costs moving with claim activity); 2 consistent 10-Q quotes; specific standard phrase, noun-only.
Reusable event/risk class (staff unionization attempts, boycotts, supply interruption); grounded in 10-K wording; class admissible despite single appearance; stable noun.

parked_rewrites.why: Record covers the notes as outstanding instruments (terms, maturity, debt composition incl. revolver context), not just the issuance event — adding 'issuance' narrows the object/mechanism.
Notes-specific name remapped to generic total-debt leverage is a re-categorization (object change from convertible notes to all indebtedness), not a spelling/word-order fix.
Evidence describes the security program managing THREATS (defense posture/training), not an experienced incident — threat vs incident is an object/mechanism change.
Evidence is the multi-concept acquisition rationale (North Italia + Flower Child + FRC, unit growth AND concept development) — not provably the Flower Child unit-growth variable the target names.

Evidence is employer-facing employment-verification regulation (hiring compliance); immigration_policy's meaning (macro policy shrinking labor pool / consumer effects) is a different object and mechanism.
Evidence is DHS/USCIS/ICE employer compliance regulation, not national immigration policy levels — object/mechanism change, not a wording fix.

Indoor dining restrictions are a strict subset of in-person dining restrictions (outdoor/patio dining was often allowed during indoor bans) — the rename widens the restriction object; evidence says 'indoor dining'.

This record's evidence is largely the combined impairment-dominated charge ($13.6M/$29.5M/$31.4M, one quote explicitly 'primarily related to the impairment of...'); stamping it 'lease_termination_costs' relabels impairment amounts as termination costs — not wording-only.
Evidence = combined pre-tax special charges ($35.6M/$24.6M impairment-dominated, plus FRC acquisition items); renaming folds a different (impairment) object under termination costs.

Evidence is a labor-line variance from higher manager headcount ('increased manager staffing levels (0.2%)'); target record means having the right GMs/exec chefs to open units — different mechanism (cost variance vs development leadership), not wording-only.

Evidence is purely a guidance sentence; the proposer's own same_as maps this record to restaurant_development_guidance — the two proposals point the same record at two different canonicals, so this rewrite (outlook folded into the activity record) is not provably wording-only.
Context shows the quote is per-unit build cost vs sales volumes (two-to-one sales-to-CapEx returns discussion), not the aggregate new-restaurant capex line — object/mechanism change.

Target name collides with an existing record whose evidence is a multi-concept 'develop, grow and operate North Italia, Flower Child and other branded concepts' risk — folding NI-only unit-growth evidence there changes scope; not wording-only.
Same collision: north_italia_development's evidence spans NI + Flower Child + other concepts and includes operating, not just unit growth; the fold changes scope.

Evidence describes the practice of leasing all restaurant locations (lease-arrangement structure), while the target record evidences the measured liability/contractual amount ($845.1M extensions) — different evidenced object, not a wording fix.

Evidence describes CAKE absorbing tariff expense in four-wall margins (company cost impact); 'tariff_policy' names the government policy object — cost-to-policy is a causal-chain hop, not a wording fix.
Evidence is the impact of a specific enacted statute (H.R. 1, July 2025); 'tax_policy' broadens scope and the target record's evidence also spans audits/litigation — generalization, not wording-only.
